session.ratification_mode: rung-1 co-draft under canon-cc-009 dual-builder tension
session.summary: First Rung 1 co-draft of the Skill Registry v0.1 interface specification. Produced under canon-cc-009 dual-Builder tension (Ashara long-arc economic voice, Petra structural floor-solid voice) following Sovereign-direct authorization conveyed through Codex session s-2026-04-20-10 on branch claude/summon-aurelius-wjnHa. The draft introduces the Province Skill primitive as sibling to canon-cc-026's Persona Skill, resolves five open forks (placement, sync model, invocation paths, transit logging, signing chain), and works the pattern end-to-end through snippet-import as the first retrofit target. Canonical spec at docs/specs/command-center/skill-registry-v0.1-draft.md. This decree is the signing-chain rationale artifact per canon-cc-027.

rung_1_rationale.voice_partition: Co-drafted with argument per canon-cc-009. Ashara carried the long-arc economic case (why-this-exists, §1; Sentinel role expansion, §6; long-arc frame, §7.7). Petra carried the structural floor-solid case (manifest schema, §2; placement consequences, §3; classification criteria, §8.1; opt-in path model, §5.4). Joint authorship on registry behavior (§4), signing chain tiers (§8), and the worked example (§9). Voice partition was co-Builders' choice; Chronicler declined to pre-partition.

authority_chain.rung_0_prompt_authorship: codex session s-2026-04-20-10 on branch claude/summon-aurelius-wjnHa

fork_resolutions.name: Placement of the canonical Province Skill
candidates: repo-root skills/ directory (first-class Province artifact, code-class)
docs/specs/skills/ (mirroring Persona Skill layout, docs-class with deploy mirror)
evidence_weighed: Persona Skills byte-mirror because companion voice must be identical across Provinces; Province Skills carry capability, not voice, and therefore do not need byte-mirroring. The reasoning that mandated docs-class placement for Persona Skills does not transfer.
Province Skills are read at runtime by sessions doing work inside the Province. Runtime consultation is a code-class interaction, not a doc-class interaction.
Placement under skills/ preserves the option of build-pipeline integration (e.g. Command Center's build.sh reading the directory for manifest validation) without mandating it at Rung 1.
The Rung 2 review surface is the whole folder (manifest + body + auxiliaries); skills/ makes the review unit coincide with the skill unit.
consequences_named: No byte-mirroring between Provinces; the skill lives once at its origin Province.
Each Province acquires a top-level skills/ directory (small cost).
Ratification artifact lives at docs/specs/skills/<name>-v<version>.md per canon-cc-027; invocable manifest lives at skills/<name>/SKILL.md. Spec-vs-instrument split documented in spec §3.2.

fork_resolutions.name: Registry sync model
candidates: pull-based: Command Center fetches each Province's skills/ via GitHub Contents API on demand
push-based: each Province emits manifests to a shared location on skill-change
static: Codex-canonical data/skills.json synchronized the way canons.json is
fork_resolutions.resolution: hybrid: Codex holds canonical data/skills.json index (records-are-Codex per cc-010); Command Center is the active Registry (indexer, renderer, invoker). Manifest bodies fetched on demand from origin Provinces.
evidence_weighed: Canon-cc-010 (records-are-Codex) places canonical records in Codex. A Registry index is a record class. Option (iii) satisfies cc-010; options (i) and (ii) do not without additional argument.
Command Center already carries the Cabinet, Consul's Chamber, Order room, Sentinel's transit log. The Registry is additive to an already-established Capital function, not displacement.
The Republic already runs this exact sync pattern for companions.json. Inventing a new model for the second instance of the same problem is Petra-anti-pattern.
Pull-based at-read-time for full manifest bodies preserves freshness without making the index itself a live-fetch dependency.
No new transport required; Ostia contract already handles this shape.
consequences_named: Two new snippet types introduced: new_skills and update_skills. Documented in spec §4.4.
Command Center is active-but-not-authoritative: if rendering disagrees with Codex record, Codex wins.
No Province writes directly to data/skills.json; all writes go through Codex snippet pipeline.

fork_resolutions.name: Primary invocation path
candidates: in-Province Claude Code session loads SKILL.md on description-match (Opus 4.7 pattern)
Command Center UI surfaces invocable skills with a run affordance
dispatch subagent owns skill-selection and invocation across Provinces
fork_resolutions.resolution: three paths, tiered. Primary = in-Province session (ships at Rung 4). Secondary = Command Center UI (ships at Capital Occupancy). Tertiary = dispatch subagent (shape-ratified only, build deferred until load justifies).
evidence_weighed: Opus 4.7 reference establishes the in-Province pattern as the pattern skills are designed to serve — skills attach to where work happens.
The Capital should exercise its own Registry, not merely index it. Secondary path prevents Registry-without-use at the Capital.
Dispatch subagent would be a solution in search of a problem at current Republic scale; honest acknowledgment prevents premature build.
Classification opt-in (invocation_paths manifest field) allows each skill to declare what it can soundly do, preserving Petra's floor-solid discipline.
consequences_named: Every skill must support in-province-session; others are opt-in.
Cross-cluster invocations must route through CC UI (canon-cc-016 boundary forbids direct cross-cluster session access).
Rung 4 ratification of the tertiary path is a shape ratification, not a build ratification; a future spec ratifies specific dispatch-subagent designs when warranted.

fork_resolutions.name: Sentinel transit log integration
candidates: skill invocations log as Ostia-class events (existing class)
skill invocations log as a new event class
skill invocations do not log at all
fork_resolutions.resolution: cross-Province skill invocations log to Sentinel's Gates-transit log as Ostia-class events. Intra-Province invocations do not log.
evidence_weighed: A cross-Province skill invocation is structurally the same event class as a deploy: capability crosses a Province boundary. Reusing the Ostia class honors the existing framing.
Logging every intra-Province invocation would flood the log with events that carry no governance weight, diluting the log's value.
Petra's discipline: instrument what matters, not what is frequent. The criterion is boundary-crossing.
Sentinel's role expands from 'archivist of deploys' to 'archivist of capability-exercise-across-Provinces' — same role, broader understanding of what constitutes a crossing.
consequences_named: Event shape defined in spec §6.1 with fields including origin_province, invoking_province, invoker_role, classification, outcome.
Cross-cluster invocations add on_behalf_of_province to record originating intent while invoking_province remains command-center (the mediator).
The log is a governance chronicle, not a performance substrate. It is not used for rate-limiting or access control — canon-cc-016 handles access at the invocation surface.

fork_resolutions.name: Signing chain for skill creation
candidates: simplify: Builder authors + Cluster Censor reviews + Sovereign ratifies
tiered: minor/internal skills run simple chain; skills crossing Provinces run full chain
fork_resolutions.resolution: tiered. Intra-Province skills run the light chain (3 rungs: Builder, Cluster Censor, Sovereign). Cross-Province skills run the full canon-cc-027 five-rung chain. Classification declared in manifest; classification itself reviewed at Rung 2.
evidence_weighed: Uniform full-chain over-burdens small capabilities that carry no cross-Province effect. The Registry's value depends on low friction for the first retrofit wave; expensive ratification for trivial skills chills adoption.
Uniform simple chain under-reviews skills that touch canonical records or cross Provinces. Under-review of cross-Province capability is the higher-risk failure.

Tiered chain pays a classification cost in exchange for right-sized review. Classification criteria (§8.1) are mechanical enough that authors make the call honestly most of the time; Rung 2 reviews the judgment.

Over-classification is recoverable (a light-tier skill reviewed at full chain is not harmed by the review). Under-classification is the failure mode the criteria are designed to prevent; 'any criterion triggers full chain' is the bias.
consequences_named: Worked table in spec §8.1 classifies the seven first-wave skills: five cross-Province, two intra-Province. Correct density for a Registry whose value is realized mostly at cross-Province invocation.
Version amendments scale with amendment class: patch = light regardless, minor = matches skill classification, major = full always. Classification changes are major amendments by definition.
Deprecation uses light chain. Deletion is separate, rare, full chain + canon-level justification.
